feed.title: HA News Feed — Pokémon GO, Brawl Stars & AI
feed.subtitle: Aggregated news: Pokémon GO, Brawl Stars & AI

🤖 SpaceX in your index fund, explained
entry.summary: Index funds are touted as one of the safest ways to invest. Rather than picking and choosing individual stocks, index funds let you bet on the market as a whole. So what happens when a company like SpaceX - a giant gamble, and, in my opinion, terribly overpriced - is fast-tracked into the Nasdaq-100

entry.title: 🤖 Get Borderlands 3, Risk of Rain 2 and 13 other great PC games for $15
entry.summary: The aptly-named “2K Megahits 2026 Bundle” from Humble includes 15 Steam games for $15. It’s a smattering of the publisher 2K’s biggest games of the 2010s, including BioShock Infinite, Risk of Rain 2, Borderlands 3, and XCOM: Enemy Unknown. The bundle will be available until August 7th, though the bu

entry.title: 🤖 The PlayStation replica ornament is an homage to a great, yet fragile console
entry.summary: You probably know the signature PlayStation boot sound. Did you know that it's technically a multi-part chime? There's the synthy section where "Sony Computer Entertainment" shows onscreen with a white background. You only get to the next screen with the echo-y chimes and the color-filled PlayStatio

entry.title: 🤖 Ford’s $30,000 electric truck: all the news about the company’s big EV re-do
entry.summary: The end of the Ford F-150 Lightning was also the start of a new era for the automaker. After failing to capture the market with the electric version of its popular F-Series truck, Ford is going back to the drawing board with its Universal Electric Vehicle Platform. The first offering will be a $30,0

entry.title: 🤖 Judge pauses Paramount’s attempt to buy Warner Bros. Discovery
entry.summary: A judge partially granted the request from a dozen state attorneys general to temporarily place the $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery on hold, as reported by Variety and Reuters. US District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín said that based on the new company's market share, "t

entry.title: 🤖 LG’s glossy OLED gaming monitor is rare to find under $400
entry.summary: If you’ve been thinking about upgrading your gaming monitor, LG’s 27-inch 27GX704A-B pairs a glossy WOLED panel with a fast refresh rate, and it’s currently on sale for $379.99 (about $70 off) at Amazon and directly from LG, marking a new low price. It originally launched at $799.99, but has been av

entry.title: 🤖 LG’s monitors come with an unwanted addition for Windows: McAfee pop-up ads
entry.summary: A video from Gamers Nexus explains how, after connecting a new LG UltraGear monitor to a PC running Windows 11 for the first time, Windows Update is silently installing LG driver updates and the LG Monitor App Installer, without so much as a permission pop-up or notification. The app doesn't appear

entry.title: 🤖 Adobe’s ‘natural look’ camera app embraces generative AI
entry.summary: Adobe's experimental camera app has taken an unexpected turn. After Project Indigo was launched last year to provide a "more natural (SLR-like) look" for iPhone photography, the Indigo camera app is now being updated with a suite of generative AI tools. And the change doesn't rely upon Adobe's own F

entry.title: 🤖 Safety and alignment in an era of long-horizon models
entry.summary: OpenAI shares lessons from deploying long-running AI models, highlighting new safety risks, observed failures, and improved safeguards through iterative deployment.

entry.title: 🤖 Following the questions where they lead
entry.summary: Assistant Professor Bailey Flanigan has arrived at complex computational methods for helping democracy thrive.

entry.title: 🤖 A scorecard for the AI age
entry.summary: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.

entry.title: 🤖 The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
entry.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none of them use today; a

entry.title: 🤖 The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
entry.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents stil

entry.title: 🤖 The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
entry.summary: Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector databases that define th

entry.title: 🤖 The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
entry.summary: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully trusts automated ev

entry.title: 🤖 Why teens deserve access to safe AI
entry.summary: Learn how OpenAI is making ChatGPT safer for teens with age-appropriate protections, learning tools, parental controls, and expert partnerships.

entry.title: 🤖 A better way to turn 2D designs into 3D models for rapid prototyping
entry.summary: Researchers developed an automated framework that helps AI models generate CAD programs more accurately and efficiently.

entry.title: 🤖 How Cars24 scales conversations and builds faster with OpenAI
entry.summary: Cars24 uses OpenAI-powered voice and chat agents to handle 1M+ monthly conversation minutes, recover 12% of lost leads, and bring agentic workflows to teams across the company.

entry.title: 🤖 Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
entry.summary: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: most deployed “agents”

entry.title: 🤖 3 Questions: Neural transparency and the future of AI design
entry.summary: Assistant Professor Pat Pataranutaporn describes a new interface that lets everyday users glimpse inside an AI's neural network before their chatbot ever says a word.

entry.title: 🤖 The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
entry.summary: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.

entry.title: 🤖 Helping AI models to meet the real world
entry.summary: Through research and entrepreneurship, Professor Devavrat Shah is helping to design methods that can handle constant decision-making using limited computational resources.

entry.title: 🤖 Can AI build a jet engine? JARVIS Challenge tests role of AI copilots in tough-tech engineering
entry.summary: MIT students designed, built, and tested a jet engine with AI copilots, assessing AI’s usefulness in developing high-performance aerospace systems.

entry.title: 🤖 How MIT students are helping to prevent cyberattacks
entry.summary: Students from the MIT Cybersecurity Clinic help local governments and other vulnerable organizations defend against digital threats.

entry.title: 🤖 AI agents create virtual playgrounds to help robots get crucial training data
entry.summary: “SceneSmith” system uses collaborative AI agents to create realistic 3D environments of places like kitchens, hotels, and living rooms, where robots can simulate everyday chores.

entry.title: 🤖 New method aims to keep kids safe from illegal AI-generated content
entry.summary: Researchers developed an auditing technique to test generative AI models for malicious capabilities, without prompting them for illegal outputs.

entry.title: 🏆 Release Notes June 2026
🏆 THE ADIDAS STARR CUP IS ON!
🏆 Brawl Stars Challengers Finals: Tickets Available Now!
🏆 THE FORMULA BRAWL EVENT IS HERE!
🤖 Google just redesigned the search box for the first time in 25 years — here’s why it matters more than you think.
entry.summary: For a quarter century, the Google search box has been one of the most recognizable interfaces in computing: a thin white rectangle, a blinking cursor, a few typed words, and a list of blue links. On Tuesday, Google will formally retire that paradigm. At its annual I/O developer conference , Google a

entry.title: 🏆 Brawl Cup Event Guide
🏆 Release Notes April 2026
🤖 Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
entry.summary: Railway , a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial intelligence applications exposes the limitations of leg
